items.title:
[负面] Overall:
The game does not respect your time, nor...
items.summary:
Overall:
The game does not respect your time, nor your money (I did not spend it and I would advise against it). You will see most of what the game has to offer strategically within about 12-15 hours. After that, farming mats IS the game. I'm surprised at how far machine translation has come to be pretty comprehensible without any human oversight, but the devs never did a human pass to clean up even the list of keywords and mechanics. Narrative is dribbled out at an agonizing pace and you are level-gated pretty hard even by chapter 3/7. If you don't mind the grind, go for it, but I had played another complete game in my library with the time I spent here.

Pros:
Somewhat original theme
Some interesting character designs
Solid music tracks
A permanent, changeable draw pool which lets you choose 1 of 4 'clans'(?) of characters to pull from
Some interesting team building considerations and combinations which give a lot of agency to the player

Cons:
Machine-translated from Korean with ZERO checking. The narrative and character dialogues are passable, somewhat stilted English, but KEYWORDS were not even standardized after the original translation pass. HUGE OVERSIGHT. This leads to inevitable fuzziness about the actual effects of some trinkets, cards, and abilities
Your characters always bring the exact same 5 cards to the fight and you don't draft/choose new cards, you can only augment slightly your main deck
Each 'level' requires 4-5 fights in story mode, taking about 15 minutes, and each chapter is 10-16 levels long. A HUGE time sink
Game progression is strictly capped by account level. I.E. you are not allowed access to material farming stages and you are not allowed to spend growth materials beyond a certain point based on your account level.
It became apparent that team building is significantly less important than the amount of growth materials you pump into your characters; any over-leveled team (regardless of synergy) will trounce lower level enemies and even a carefully considered and perfectly played composition will lose to enemies that are marginally higher level than you.

Other notes:
Did not get to try the multiplayer simplified card game, which looked interesting. However, you can only use characters and items you've unlocked to play, so make of that what you will.

items.title: [正面] TLDR: Just try the game out! It's free and it's fu...
items.summary:
TLDR: Just try the game out! It's free and it's fun!

This gotta be one of my most favorite Gacha game out of the many I have played, It is just that good.
This game is EXTREMELY generous and F2P friendly and I am NOT exaggerating when I say that.
And to my surprise the turn-based card combat is actually quite unique and very engaging (a little too complex but that's a good thing if you understand all the mechanics)

The only huge downside Is that all of the translation and stuffs are all done by ChatGPT which is a double edged sword: There's no annoying localization like putting some bs political stuffs in it instead of a 1 to 1 translation. ChatGPT can get a little quirky at times but I don't even think that's a bad thing lol (It makes the dialog kinda funny) But for new players trying read and understand the mechanics of the game, it is definitely a problem.
